comment:
This plugin is essential yet has so many broken features:

  • Constant lock ups of whatever Jetbrains IDE you're using. Jetbrains will flag this component as causing performance problems constantly.
  • Ignores whether you're in Agent mode or Ask mode (sometimes Agent requests will get spit into Chat instead of acted upon).

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Fundamentally broken.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
This is by far the worst agent experience I've had with any of the tools:

  • Every time I start a new chat, multiple old and unrelated files are opened in the editor
  • I don't see any option to completely disable the automatic "add file to context" feature. I have to turn them off manually every time. I do not want to automatically add files to the context. This has led to unrelated files being added to the context numerous times which is a huge waste of tokens and context space. Just give me an option to disable this feature entirely, especially in agent mode.
  • The agent is still unable to read the terminal output most of the time
  • For about a week the model's output is missing characters, making it almost impossible to properly read what the model is telling me
  • I do not see any option to show me how full the current context is. This is super relevant to effectively work with the agent
  • I did not manage to configure skills in a way where the agent picks them up reliably
  • The feature to accept/decline edits made by the agent triggers so many edge cases it's hilarious. Make the agent change a file and then make the agent move it using a terminal command? The Changes are gone. Numerous bugs with the accept/decline buttons not rendering properly or rendering on top of completely unrelated UI elements (even on top of other/unrelated applications)
  • Configuring auto accepts for certain terminal calls is extremely cumbersome and hard to debug. I have not found any documentation for that and was forced to enable intellij logs to find out what the problem was.
  • When asked to accept a terminal command, there is no easy way to say "allow all uses of...". I have to open the settings (which is a modal, btw. I can't change the settings and use the agent at the same time) and manually add some regex to the table of allowed terminal commands. This is super cumbersome.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: How it's still so crap after so long is beyond me... Please introduce negative star reviews. 2 star average is too high.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
I think that Github Copilot is getting decent now. Everything works properly, but I'm still missing some features and there's some improvements that could help:

  1. In PLAN mode, will be much better if we can answer the questions selecting something, or a better way, because when I plan something and answer some questions, the agent tries to start editing, using tokens to it.
  2. We can see how many tokens the context are using, to know when its better to reset the context.
  3. Some way to natively interate loops and tasks. Idk how the PLAN or AGENT mode works, but now there's no clear way to track the progress.
  4. Sometimes it tries to access some access denied directory and tries to run "cat" commands, and this is bad. I tried a workaround, but its not the best way.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: I am fed up with the constant technical glitches / pitfalls in Copilot; it’s making my work impossible. I have been using Copilot since mid 2025, and it worked perfectly. After new releases in 2026, it becomes impossible to use. Basically, it freezes DataSpell completely when I'm trying to open Copilot/or write something there, and the only solution is to kill DataSpell. Now I switched to the previous release of Copilot 1.5.62-243 together with DataSpell 2025.2.3, and it works more or less better, still running intensively CPU when I'm trying to write in Copilot chat.

comment:
Overall useful but the main pain points are :

  • impossible to copy code from the prompt result with Ctrl-C (why ??!!), you have to hit right-click>Copy like a boomer.
  • no chat history or possibility to navigate through prompts (like a jetbrains evaluate Alt-F8 / arrows). This is particularly painful when the prompt issues an error and you have to resubmit but since your prompt is always deleted after a search you have to retype it from scratch. Of course there's no context history and if you just ask "please reprocess my previous prompt" it's completely lost...

This plugin is really bad, it crashes a lot of times, my IDE has started to not responding every now and then and I'm sure it's due to this plugin.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: Just a really buggy plugin at this point. I've had it just throw away or ignore my prompts so often that I now compose them in a separate editor. Also, switching modes (eg, Ask -> Edit) is apparently an entirely different thing, so it loses all your context including files added and whatnot. It's pretty stark how much worse this is than Claude Code, even when using the same underlying LLM models. Which is unfortunate, it seems like the potential is there for it to be better, but instead it's just a buggy mess.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: It works, but it's not usable and may be a good enough reason at this point for switching from PyCharm to VS Code altogether. It's sad that this doesn't get more attention to get it on par with what VS code can do, or at least close to it. Why is auto-approve not working? it's annoying to get prompted for every little thing to continue. The text editor is broken: it doesn't handle multi lines properly, it acts like one single line. Pressing end will not go to the end of line, but instead at the end of the whole text. When adding new lines at the end, it takes a fragment from the last part and there's no way to really navigate to the end. It's riddled with bugs overall.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment: I like the plugin, but there are some things that are really annoying. For instance it cannot read the console all the time. Sometimes it works flawlessly and couple of minutes later it loops same things in cli so i have to stop it, copy-paste the output into agent chat and then it continues normally. Also i have a feeling that models are dumbed down for some reason. I use cursor in parallel because of this. Claude 4.6 in cursor behaves much better than same model in Copilot. Things that Claude does fast and properly in Cursor, Copilot version reasons completely different and needs more time to do it right. There must be something in the implementation of models that causes behavior like this.

plugin.name: GitHub Copilot - Your AI Pair Programmer

comment:
GitHub Copilot for PhpStorm – Great Idea, Rough Execution

★★☆☆☆

I really wanted to love this plugin. The concept is solid, and when it works, it genuinely helps with day-to-day development. But after a few weeks of daily use in PhpStorm, the list of frustrations has grown long enough that I feel obligated to warn others.

Terminal blindness The most baffling issue: Copilot Chat happily runs commands in the terminal — php -l, cd, you name it — but then acts as if nothing happened. It executes the command and completely ignores the output, leaving you to manually copy-paste results back into the chat just to continue the conversation. For an AI assistant that's supposed to streamline your workflow, this is a pretty fundamental failure. Agent mode helps slightly, but it's inconsistent.

Text selection goes haywire Selecting code in the editor while the Copilot panel is active is an exercise in frustration. The selection frequently jumps, shifts, or ends up somewhere entirely unintended. Simple actions like highlighting a method or a block of code become unreliable, which in a tool you use dozens of times per hour is genuinely disruptive.

The cursor teleports Related to the above — the plugin has a habit of moving the editor cursor to seemingly random locations on its own. You're working in one file, Copilot does something in the background, and suddenly your view or cursor position has jumped somewhere else entirely. It breaks focus and flow constantly.

System freezes This one is the dealbreaker. Periodically — and unpredictably — the plugin freezes the entire IDE and sometimes the whole system for 20 to 40 seconds. No warning, no spinner, just a complete lockup. On a machine that handles Docker, Kubernetes and multiple services without breaking a sweat, this is hard to excuse. It appears to happen most often when the chat is processing or when switching between files rapidly.

Bottom line The underlying AI capabilities are promising, and I can see where this is headed. But right now the plugin feels like it shipped too early. The terminal integration is broken by design, the editor interactions are glitchy, and the freezes make it actively harmful to productivity at times. I'll revisit when these basics are fixed — until then, I can't recommend it for serious daily use.

comment:
Overall, the plugin is really good. They just have a quality control issue with their releases.

In DataGrip 2025.3.5, I had auto-update turned on, and when the newest version of the Copilot plugin 1.5.65-243 (from 2026/02/12) got installed, the plugin crashed on startup. I turned off auto-update, and downgraded to version 1.5.63-243 (from 2020/01/15) using the "Install from disk" process, and everything is working again.
